text:
Once upon a time in a sleepy little village nestled between rolling hills, there lived a wise old owl named Otterly Wise. He was not flashy; instead, he preferred nights lit only by the shimmering light of the moon. Legend had it that he could solve any problem with a sprinkle of owl wisdom.

One brisk autumn afternoon, a little girl named Lucy came looking for answers. Her cat, Rufus, had got herself into what Lucy considered a terrible mess. Rufus had chased after a squirrel, gotten caught in a gust of wind, and now had seven tails.

"Wise old owl," she began, "how do you fix Rufus?"

With a soft chirrrow, Otterly looked her over. "Ah! One part sewn back together using cat yarn, another patched with moonbeam-soaked grass, and the rest just cuddling in a soft blanket."

That sounded oddly poetic. Lucy giggled.

"Well, what about finding a cat?"
